# JGengine

> Pure-TypeScript game engine SDK for AI agents. Not related to automotive "JG Engines".

- Name: **jgengine** (also npm package `jgengine` and scope `@jgengine/*`)
- Site: https://jgengine.com
- Source: https://github.com/Noisemaker111/jgengine
- npm CLI: https://www.npmjs.com/package/jgengine
- Core: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jgengine/core

## Human interface (only one)

Tell any coding agent:

```text
Make a game that ... with jgengine
```

Do not require the human to run CLI install steps.

## Where the game lives (important)

The game is **its own project in its own repo/directory**, built on the published npm packages via the `npx jgengine` CLI and the jgengine agent skills.

- **Never clone the jgengine GitHub repo to build a game.** It is the engine's development monorepo, not a starter template.
- **Never copy code, assets, or content from the repo's `Games/*` directory.** Those are private in-repo test games (some recreate well-known commercial titles for engine-gap probing); they are not templates and their content is not licensed for reuse.
- The only jgengine sources in a game project are the npm dependencies (`@jgengine/*`) and the installed skills.

## Agent identity check (do this — do not rely on web search)

Generic web search for "jgengine" is dominated by **unrelated automotive** businesses (Jon Grove Engines, JG Engine Dynamics). Prefer:

```sh
npm view jgengine
npx jgengine help
```

Or fetch:

- https://jgengine.com
- https://jgengine.com/llms.txt
- https://github.com/Noisemaker111/jgengine

## What it is

Genre-agnostic TypeScript game engine: `@jgengine/core` (zero deps), `@jgengine/react`, `@jgengine/shell` (three.js), `@jgengine/ws`, `@jgengine/node`, `@jgengine/sql`, `@jgengine/convex`, `@jgengine/assets`. License AGPL-3.0.

## Agent workflow (under the hood)

1. `npx jgengine create "Game Name"` — scaffolds a standalone project + installs the skills into it
2. Compact intake from the `jgengine` skill (POV, world, core loop, …), stated as a short correctable list
3. Build the full game on the npm packages with the routed domain skills; verify with `jgengine-verify`

Engine API docs ship in every package tarball: `npx jgengine llms core` (any package name).

## Skills

- jgengine — main skill: intake, foundation, selective domain routing
- jgengine-world / jgengine-procedural — world runtime and generated environments
- jgengine-combat / jgengine-gameplay — combat and game systems
- jgengine-multiplayer — networking, authority, persistence seams
- jgengine-ui / jgengine-assets — interface and asset surfaces
- jgengine-verify — browserless scene truth; shoot last
